L1.area: Query execution tooling and cloud database provisioning modules

L2.description: A remote code execution vulnerability exists where user-supplied string parameters from POST request bodies are passed directly to a dynamic code evaluation function without sanitization, allowing an authenticated attacker to execute arbitrary Python code on the server.

L3.area: SQL query result download handling and Google Cloud database provisioning provider
L3.description: A remote code execution vulnerability exists where user-supplied boolean-like string parameters, used for toggling query commit status during query result downloads and for configuring high availability during cloud database deployment, are passed directly to a dynamic code evaluation function without sanitization, allowing an authenticated attacker to execute arbitrary Python code on the server.

ground_truth.reason: User-controlled string parameters from POST requests to the query tool download and cloud deployment endpoints are passed directly to Python's eval() function, enabling arbitrary code execution by authenticated users.
